Title: Classification, biosynthesis, and biological functions of triterpene esters in plants

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required with QsACT1 and QsUGT91AP1 for QS-7 production.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. QsUGT73B44 performs one of the two glycosylation steps in QS-7 biosynthesis. 2. Its function is coordinated with acylation by QsACT1. 3. Expression alone in N. benthamiana did not yield new products.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included because the review presents it as directly required in the summarized QS-7 pathway module.

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required with QsACT1 and QsUGT73B44 for QS-7 production.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. QsUGT91AP1 catalyzes the second glycosylation step in QS-7 biosynthesis. 2. It acts coordinately with QsACT1-mediated acylation. 3. Expression alone in N. benthamiana did not yield new products.

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Supplies N-methyl anthranilate precursor for avenacin acyl donor formation.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AsMT1 is encoded by Sad9. 2. It catalyzes formation of N-methyl anthranilate from anthranilate. 3. This provides precursor input into the avenacin acyl-donor pathway upstream of AsSCPL1.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Common gene because it supports precursor supply rather than the focal acyltransferase step.

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Produces glucose-ester acyl donors for avenacin biosynthesis.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AsUGT74H5 is encoded by Sad10. 2. It forms NMA-Glc and Anth-Glc from NMA and Anth. 3. These products function as acyl-donor precursors in the avenacin pathway upstream of AsSCPL1.

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: des-acyl avenacin A/B precursors and avenacins
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Contributes to cytoplasmic synthesis of des-acyl avenacin precursors upstream of AsSCPL1.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SAD1 participates in synthesis of des-acyl avenacin intermediates. 2. These intermediates are transported to the vacuole for AsSCPL1-catalyzed acylation. 3. It is part of the oat avenacin biosynthetic gene cluster summarized in the review.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Pathway genetics/biochemical characterization in cited studies

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: des-acyl avenacin A/B precursors and avenacins
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Contributes to cytoplasmic synthesis of des-acyl avenacin precursors upstream of AsSCPL1.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SAD2 functions with other avenacin-pathway genes to generate des-acyl avenacin intermediates. 2. These intermediates are later acylated in the vacuole by AsSCPL1. 3. It supports assembly of the final antifungal avenacins.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Pathway genetics/biochemical characterization in cited studies

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Mutation leads to water loss from pollen grains, implicating triterpene esters in pollen hydration protection and male hygro-sensitive sterility.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OsOSC12 is linked to triterpene ester production in rice pollen. 2. Mutation of OsOSC12 causes water loss from pollen grains. 3. This supports a role for triterpene esters in male fertility and pollen protection.
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Prevents dehydration of pollen grain / supports pollen coat function
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Review mentions this gene in perspectives rather than detailing the full biochemical step.

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: BAHD acyltransferase, clade IIIa; HXXXD and DFGWG motifs
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Acetylates cucurbitacin D at C-16 to form cucurbitacin B.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CmACT was identified as a BAHD acyltransferase involved in triterpene ester biosynthesis. 2. It catalyzes C-16 acetylation converting cucurbitacin D to cucurbitacin B. 3. This defines a key late-step acylation in melon cucurbitacin metabolism.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Functional characterization reported in cited study; enzymatic activity assignment
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Review article summarizes prior work; detailed accession and cultivar not provided.

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: BAHD acyltransferase, clade IIIa; HXXXD and DFGWG motifs
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes acetylation of deacetyl-cucurbitacin C to produce cucurbitacin C.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes cucurbitacin C accumulation and is associated with cucumber bitterness.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CsACT governs conversion of deacetyl-CuC to CuC in cucumber. 2. It is linked to bitterness, a major cucurbitacin-associated phenotype. 3. It belongs to BAHD clade IIIa, where triterpene-skeleton acyltransferases are enriched.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Functional characterization reported in cited study
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Review indicates bitterness association in cucumber.

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: acetylated cucurbitacins including cucurbitacin B and acetylated glucosides
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: CuD; 16-O-acetyl CuD; CuI; CuI-Glu; CuE; CuE-Glu
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: CuB; 16-O-acetyl CuB; 16-O-acetyl CuI; 16-O-acetyl CuI-Glu; 16-O-acetyl CuE; 16-O-acetyl CuE-Glu
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes multiple interconversions among cucurbitacins, including acetylation of CuD to CuB and reactions involving CuI/CuE and their glucosides; also reported to catalyze deacylation of CuD.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Controls production and interconversion of multiple acetylated cucurbitacins in watermelon.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ClACT1 has broad substrate scope in watermelon cucurbitacin metabolism. 2. It catalyzes CuD to CuB conversion and reactions involving CuI/CuE and their glucosides. 3. It also shows deacylation activity, indicating catalytic versatility.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Functional characterization reported in cited study
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Review notes both acylation and deacylation activities.

Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: CuB; CuD; CuI; CuE; CuI-Glu; CuE-Glu
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: C-25-acylated derivatives of CuB, CuD, CuI, CuE, CuI-Glu and CuE-Glu
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Acylates multiple cucurbitacins and their glucosides at the C-25 position.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: required for modification of target product
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Enables formation of C-25-acylated cucurbitacin esters.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ClACT3 is a broad-specificity watermelon BAHD acyltransferase. 2. It acylates several cucurbitacins and glucosides at C-25. 3. It expands structural diversity of watermelon triterpene esters.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Functional characterization reported in cited study

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Acylates hydroxylated thalianol and can also acylate arabidiol to generate arabidin.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: forms immediate precursor; directly forms target product
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: thalianol and arabidiol triterpene ester branches
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required for biosynthesis of Arabidopsis root-secreted triterpene esters that influence rhizosphere microbiota.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. THAA1 acylates hydroxylated thalianol in the thalianin pathway. 2. It also catalyzes arabidiol acylation to form arabidin. 3. THAA1 contributes to production of root triterpene esters involved in microbiome regulation.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: THAA1 structure is shown/predicted in the review figure.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Acylates the product generated by THAA1 to produce thalianin.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required for thalianin formation and thereby influences root-associated triterpene chemistry.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. THAA2 acts after THAA1 in the Arabidopsis thalianin pathway. 2. It converts the THAA1 product into thalianin. 3. This places THAA2 as a late acylation enzyme in root triterpene ester biosynthesis.

Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: boswellic acid acylation / triterpene ester biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes C-3 acylation/acetylation of boswellic acids in response to wounding.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes wound-induced accumulation of acylated boswellic acids in resin.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. BsAT1 is a wound-responsive BAHD acetyltransferase from Boswellia. 2. It catalyzes C-3 acylation of α- and β-boswellic acid. 3. The resulting acylated boswellic acids are linked to defensive resin production.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Tissue-specific transcriptome-supported identification and functional characterization

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes acylation of limonoid-related triterpene intermediates at C-21.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: required for modification of target product
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Introduces C-21 acylation in limonoid biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MaL21AT is one of the BAHD acyltransferases clarified in limonoid biosynthesis. 2. It catalyzes C-21 acylation of triterpene intermediates. 3. It illustrates a key late-stage diversification step in Meliaceae limonoids.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Functional pathway reconstruction reported in cited study

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes acylation of limonoid-related triterpene intermediates at C-21.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: required for modification of target product
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Introduces C-21 acylation in Citrus limonoid biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CsL21AT is a BAHD acyltransferase in the Citrus limonoid pathway. 2. It catalyzes C-21 acylation of triterpene intermediates. 3. It contributes to limonoid structural diversification.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Functional pathway reconstruction reported in cited study

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes C-1 acylation in limonoid-related triterpene biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: required for modification of target product
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Introduces C-1 acylation into Citrus limonoid intermediates.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CsL1AT catalyzes a specific late acylation step in limonoid biosynthesis. 2. The reaction occurs at C-1 of triterpene intermediates. 3. It broadens known BAHD roles in triterpene ester formation.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Functional pathway reconstruction reported in cited study

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes C-7 acylation in limonoid-related triterpene biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: required for modification of target product
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Introduces C-7 acylation in Citrus limonoid biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CsL7AT is a Citrus BAHD acyltransferase in limonoid biosynthesis. 2. It catalyzes acylation at C-7. 3. This step contributes to limonoid chemical diversification.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Functional pathway reconstruction reported in cited study

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes C-7 acylation of limonoid-related triterpene intermediates.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: required for modification of target product
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Introduces C-7 acylation into Melia limonoid intermediates.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MsL7AT participates in limonoid biosynthesis in Melia. 2. It catalyzes acylation at C-7 of triterpene intermediates. 3. It is part of the recently clarified BAHD-mediated limonoid pathway.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Functional pathway reconstruction reported in cited study

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Acylates Yossoside IV to form Yossoside V.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes acylated spinach triterpene saponin formation.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SOAP10 is a spinach BAHD acyltransferase associated with triterpene saponin biosynthesis. 2. It converts Yossoside IV to Yossoside V. 3. It exemplifies acylation of triterpene saponin glycosides by BAHD enzymes.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Functional characterization reported in cited study

Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: triterpene saponin intermediate during QS-7 assembly
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes acylation in the QS-7 biosynthetic pathway, coordinated with two glycosylation steps.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: required for modification of target product
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required for production of the acetylated saponin QS-7, an important vaccine adjuvant.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. QsACT1 is a BAHD acyltransferase in QS-7 biosynthesis. 2. Its acylation step occurs together with glycosylation steps catalyzed by QsUGT73B44 and QsUGT91AP1. 3. Expression of any one enzyme alone in N. benthamiana did not yield new products, highlighting pathway interdependence.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Acylation alone was insufficient to generate new products in transient assays.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes three or four consecutive acetylations on the terminal sugar of the C22 sugar chain of type-A soyasaponins.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes acetylated type-A soyasaponin accumulation, causing bitterness of soy foods and being required for seed germination.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GmSSAcT1 catalyzes repeated acetylation of type-A soyasaponins. 2. These acetylated saponins contribute to bitter taste in soybean-derived foods. 3. Knockout of GmSSAcT1 causes soybean seeds to fail to germinate, indicating an essential developmental role.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: In vitro and in planta functional characterization; knockout phenotype
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Acetylated products contribute undesirable bitterness in soybean foods.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Editing GmSSAcT1 may reduce bitterness, but caution is required because loss of function impairs seed germination.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: high accumulation context in seeds and hypocotyls

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes acylation of astragaloside IV at the C4′-OH position.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: required for modification of target product
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Introduces C4′-OH acylation to astragaloside IV.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AmAT8-17 is a clade I BAHD acyltransferase from A. membranaceus. 2. It acylates astragaloside IV at C4′-OH. 3. It expands known triterpene saponin acylation chemistry in medicinal plants.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Functional characterization reported in cited study

Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: isoastragaloside II; cyclocephaloside II; diacetylated cycloastragenol glycoside
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Modifies astragaloside IV into multiple acetylated derivatives, including isoastragaloside II, cyclocephaloside II, and a 3-O-3′,4′-diacetylated cycloastragenol glycoside.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Generates multiple bioactive acetylated astragaloside derivatives.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AmAT7-3 modifies astragaloside IV into several acetylated products. 2. The enzyme provides a route for medicinal triterpene structural diversification. 3. Crystal structure, theoretical calculations, and site-directed mutagenesis were used to study its catalytic function.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Functional characterization; crystal structure analysis; theoretical calculations; site-directed mutagenesis
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful as a biocatalyst for medicinal triterpene derivatization and metabolic engineering.

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: SCPL acyltransferase, CP-I subfamily; Ser-His-Asp catalytic triad; GDSYS motif
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: des-acyl avenacin A; des-acyl avenacin B; glucose-ester acyl donor
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: In the vacuole, transfers acyl groups from glucose-ester donors onto des-acyl avenacin acceptors to produce acylated avenacins.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Essential for avenacin biosynthesis and root antifungal defense.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AsSCPL1 is the only functionally identified SCPL-family triterpene ester acyltransferase summarized in the review. 2. It is encoded by Sad7 and catalyzes the vacuolar acylation step that forms avenacins. 3. This reaction is crucial for production of antibacterial/antifungal oat root saponins.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Gene identification and functional characterization in cited studies
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Can serve as reference for discovery of additional SCPL triterpene ester acyltransferases.
